description: Thin horizontal or vertical divider. When decorative={true} (the default), Radix renders role="none" so assistive tech ignores it. When decorative={false}, it renders role="separator" with aria-orientation so screen readers can announce the boundary.

announcements.With decorative={false}: announces as a separator with its orientation.

screenReader.notes: Use decorative={false} only when the separator carries real semantic meaning (e.g. between two unrelated lists or sections); purely visual lines should stay decorative to avoid clutter in the AT output.

contrast.ui: Uses bg-border; meets AA non-text contrast against bg-background

consumerResponsibilities: Keep decorative={true} unless the separator meaningfully divides content; otherwise screen reader users hear extra noise.
Prefer semantic headings over separators when grouping content; separators complement headings but don't replace them.
